faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Featured on game cover with Jason Giambi as primary visual identity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team uniforms and color schemes differentiate home/away play
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stadium environments vary by team affiliation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Solo player character on field with AI-controlled opponents
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI teams execute defensive and offensive plays autonomously

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Calendar/schedule interface showing progression through multiple games
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Team rosters and standings update based on accumulated results
Elimination mechanics; loss results in season termination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Specialized arena with distance markers and scoring display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Power-hitting focus with ball trajectory visualization

environmental_storytelling.example: Team selection and transfers come under player control, allowing customization of roster composition to reflect strategic intent
Robust creation system allows visual identity construction of custom baseball players with appearance and ability differentiation
Real MLB players (Jason Giambi featured) provide authentic team and league identity through recognizable athlete likenesses

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stance and swing animation vary based on player type and situation context
Positioning and movement respond to ball trajectory and game situation
Executes strategic plays autonomously during single-player and season modes

notes: Triple Play Baseball (2001) is a sports simulation with limited narrative or political tension elements typical of the sports paradigm. Visual communication focuses on team identity through roster management, uniform differentiation, and stadium representation rather than danger/safety signals or faction conflict. The game emphasizes gameplay mode states (single player, season, playoffs, home run derby) as primary world-state communicators. The Wikipedia source provided review aggregation data rather than detailed manifestation mechanics; analysis relies on inferred gameplay systems from feature descriptions.
